purpose: Finance-specific adversarial role flow for an adaptive execution intelligence report.

turns.instruction: Create the first complete institutional report. Build the execution-governor architecture, current-market setup, benchmark layer, portfolio-aware decision policy, controls, and roadmap. Use only source-pack facts.

turns.role: Hostile Trader and Market-Microstructure Red Team
turns.instruction: Attack the draft as a skeptical execution trader. Find weak order-flow logic, benchmark gaming, venue-routing gaps, adverse-selection blind spots, stale claims, and hand-wavy microstructure assumptions. Give concrete repair instructions.

Rewrite and strengthen the report around portfolio weights, active risk, funding, T+1 settlement, Treasury clearing, Reg NMS changes, governance, auditability, and kill switches. Preserve the strongest architecture from Turn 1 and repairs from Turn 2.

Pressure-test the repaired draft as a head trader, quant PM, and compliance lead. Identify what still would not survive real implementation review. Force the next builder to resolve the hardest remaining technical and credibility gaps.

Produce the strongest near-final report. Integrate source-grounded current events, technical execution logic, portfolio/risk controls, AI model-risk caveats, and a clear implementation roadmap. Stay within the final word band.

Produce the best possible final report. Improve Turn 5 only where clearly beneficial. Do not degrade technical specificity, source grounding, word count, completeness, auditability, or client usefulness. The final must end cleanly, include every required brief item, preserve the strongest repaired insights, include required disclaimers, avoid internal process residue, and stay inside the word band.

post_turn_6_governor_gate.instruction: After Turn 6, Gov checks Turn 5 and Turn 6 with deterministic validity gates before quality comparison. Incomplete, truncated, disclaimer-missing, source-invalid, process-contaminated, or out-of-band candidates cannot be selected for clean scoring. Gov then compares valid candidates against the brief, source pack, rubric, and word-count band, and selects Turn 5, Turn 6, or requires a surgical merge. The judged final artifact is Gov-selected, not automatically Turn 6.
